title: Buildah bud --layers does not provide shorter time for parallel builds of consequent containers that share some common base
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

The ``--layers`` option is only useful when building *exactly the same* containers but using different target directories.
Consequent container builds that share some common base could not benefit from that layers cache. Which is quite suboptimal.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run concurrent ``buildah bud --layers --rm=false`` builds for multiple containers that share some common base layer
2. Compare the time it takes to the same operation executed without ``--layers --rm=false``
3. (probably expected behavior) If specifying only ``--layers`` without ``--rm=false``, it likely fails some builds with errors like ``error checking if cached image exists from a previous build: error getting history of "<id>": error creating new image from reference to image "<id>": error locating item named "manifest" for image with ID``

**Describe the results you received:**
The time it takes to build ~40 quite fat and multi-layered containers is roughly 30 minutes for the above scenarios 1 and 2.
I have the same results with buildah built from trunk master (v1.11.6-830-g31b0c5db).

See more of the similar results with using ``--layers --rm=false`` [0]
```
2020-11-12 20:05:44.891116 | TASK [build-containers : Run image build as ansible user > /home/zuul/workspace/build.log]
2020-11-12 20:36:24.390268 | primary | ok: Runtime: 0:30:38.402444
```
without ``--layers`` [1]
```
2020-11-10 01:33:15.162933 | TASK [build-containers : Run image build as ansible user > /home/zuul/workspace/build.log]
2020-11-10 01:58:09.258823 | primary | ok: Runtime: 0:24:53.062323
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
With --layers it should complete faster because of the image cache benefits.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
N/A (built locally from trunk)
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.18.0-dev (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.5 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux thinkpad 4.15.0-122-generic #124-Ubuntu SMP Thu Oct 15 13:03:05 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

[0] https://storage.gra.cloud.ovh.net/v1/AUTH_dcaab5e32b234d56b626f72581e3644c/zuul_opendev_logs_e62/762561/1/check/tripleo-build-containers-ubi-8/e621705/job-output.txt

[1] https://storage.bhs.cloud.ovh.net/v1/AUTH_dcaab5e32b234d56b626f72581e3644c/zuul_opendev_logs_b36/761996/1/check/tripleo-build-containers-ubi-8/b364a30/job-output.txt

comments.body:
Are you seeing the layers being removed when --rm=false is set?

If you do 
```
# buildah bud --layers --rm=false /tmp/test1
# buildah bud --layers --rm=false /tmp/test2
```
Where test1 and test2 share the same first couple of command 
layers 
Does the second cont show that it is using cache?

@rhatdan I'm not figured out yet how layers got removed and how things get logged for that. But using that linked above debug patch https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2785, I noticed another thing: the image cache info struct "changes" its keys on fly. Is that expected to happen? E.g.: https://pastebin.com/zfjLEewA

Update: when I see some "Using cache foo" in logs and then search for it, it finds such layers:

sudo find /var -name '*foo*'
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/foo
So that part of the pusszle should be fine. Would be nice to modify my patch to have the removals of layers logged as well

So, If I do

```
# buildah bud --layers --rm=false /tmp/test1
# buildah bud --layers --rm=false /tmp/test2
```
Where test1 and test2 share the same first couple of command
layers
The second cont shows that it is using cache.

But when I build another image based on that test1, just adding extra layers on top, there is no any use of cache.
This bug is exactly about the latter.

I am not seeing it, here is my simple test

```
# cat /tmp/test1/Containerfile 
from alpine
RUN echo hello
RUN echo goodbye
RUN echo test1

# cat /tmp/test2/Containerfile 
from alpine
RUN echo hello
RUN echo goodbye
RUN echo test2
```

```
# buildah bud --layers /tmp/test1
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN echo hello
hello
--> bc83efda254
STEP 3: RUN echo goodbye
goodbye
--> 62a07f2418f
STEP 4: RUN echo test1
test1
STEP 5: COMMIT
--> 2db664a4bae
2db664a4bae419db1dc80a432f1f48bd5808845a6aac55b559bb2222ee93fe9b

# buildah bud --layers /tmp/test2
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN echo hello
--> Using cache bc83efda2548b646db0ff7c607150e53ee785c96b2285a274618a5ef1316aaa7
--> bc83efda254
STEP 3: RUN echo goodbye
--> Using cache 62a07f2418f8b560e74ca9322a67dbd1e9b6e319b7ddda6361792f5ab36fdddf
--> 62a07f2418f
STEP 4: RUN echo test2
test2
STEP 5: COMMIT
--> 144f7c78655
144f7c78655563fe6f1ca1bbc4bc1082d384fbc926aa5cf58409e7b885b52737

# buildah bud --layers /tmp/test1
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN echo hello
--> Using cache bc83efda2548b646db0ff7c607150e53ee785c96b2285a274618a5ef1316aaa7
--> bc83efda254
STEP 3: RUN echo goodbye
--> Using cache 62a07f2418f8b560e74ca9322a67dbd1e9b6e319b7ddda6361792f5ab36fdddf
--> 62a07f2418f
STEP 4: RUN echo test1
--> Using cache 2db664a4bae419db1dc80a432f1f48bd5808845a6aac55b559bb2222ee93fe9b
--> 2db664a4bae
2db664a4bae419db1dc80a432f1f48bd5808845a6aac55b559bb2222ee93fe9b
```

Ok thank you, I can confirm that also works the same way for me.
The confusion was in how ``--layers`` could help with building ``test2...N`` containers that depend on ``test1``. It seems it cannot. So I'm closing this with a conclusion that ``--layers`` works as designed. And is only useful when building *exactly the same containers* but using different targets.

My testing in tripleo for openstack builds was using older version of buildah where was no use of cache, hence this issue.
While retesting it with latest build, I can see there is use of cache in the same scenario. So this bug is invalid, so is my comment as well that layers cache is only efficient when building *exactly the same* images as different targets. Testing shows it is efficient even when building images that use other layers, like COPY foo, on top of the common base.
